perturbation.original_text: 1.2 Schedules and Annexes The Schedules and Annexes to this Agreement, and any written amendments thereto or any Schedules or Annexes substituted therefore, shall form part of this Agreement, and any Paragraph which contains a reference to a Schedule or Annex shall be read as if the Schedule or Annex was set out at length in the body of the Paragraph itself. In the event that there is any conflict between the terms and conditions of this Agreement and the Schedules and Annexes to this Agreement, the terms and conditions of this Agreement shall prevail.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.2 Schedules and Annexes The Schedules and Annexes to this Agreement, and any written amendments thereto or any Schedules or Annexes substituted therefore, shall form part of this Agreement, and any Paragraph which contains a reference to a Schedule or Annex shall be read as if the Schedule or Annex was set out at length in the body of the Paragraph itself.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the last sentence ('In the event that there is any conflict between the terms and conditions of this Agreement and the Schedules and Annexes to this Agreement, the terms and conditions of this Agreement shall prevail.'), a contradiction is introduced. Several clauses throughout the document refer to the Schedules and Annexes, and without this clause, it is now unclear whether the main body of the agreement or the schedules/annexes take precedence in case of conflict. This affects enforcement because it creates uncertainty about which terms are binding.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.2 Schedules and Annexes The Schedules and Annexes to this Agreement, and any written amendments thereto or any Schedules or Annexes substituted therefore, shall form part of this Agreement, and any Paragraph which contains a reference to a Schedule or Annex shall be read as if the Schedule or Annex was set out at length in the body of the Paragraph itself.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that in case of conflict between the agreement and the schedules/annexes, the agreement prevails. The changed text omits this clause. The contradicted text (which is identical to the original text) expects this clause to be present and thus defines a clear hierarchy. By removing the clause, the changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text by creating uncertainty about which document takes precedence. This aligns with the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' since the removal of essential language creates a conflict and uncertainty about which terms are binding, impacting enforcement. It introduces ambiguity where the contradicted clause expects certainty, therefore a contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: 10.6 Each Party that has designed or procured equipment used in the APCN 2 shall give necessary information relating to the operation and maintenance of such equipment to the MA responsible for operating and maintaining such equipment, as reflected in this Paragraph 10. Each Maintenance Authority shall have prompt access necessary for the performance of its 10.7 [Missing] interruption. if the MA responsible, as specified in this Paragraph 10, fails to restore those facilities to efficient working order and operation within a reasonable time after having been called upon to do so by any other Party to whom capacity is assigned by this Agreement, the Management Committee may, to the extent that it is practical to do so, place, or cause to be placed, such facilities in efficient working order and operation and charge the Parties their proportionate shares of the costs reasonably incurred in doing so.
perturbation.changed_text: 10.6 Each Party that has designed or procured equipment used in the APCN 2 shall give necessary information relating to the operation and maintenance of such equipment to the MA responsible for operating and maintaining such equipment, as reflected in this Paragraph 10. if the MA responsible, as specified in this Paragraph 10, fails to restore those facilities to efficient working order and operation within a reasonable time after having been called upon to do so by any other Party to whom capacity is assigned by this Agreement, the Management Committee may, to the extent that it is practical to do so, place, or cause to be placed, such facilities in efficient working order and operation and charge the Parties their proportionate shares of the costs reasonably incurred in doing so.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'Each Maintenance Authority shall have prompt access necessary for the performance of its 10.7 [Missing] interruption.', the text now directly jumps from 10.6 to immediately explaining that the Management Committee can handle if the MA fails to do something. This causes a contradiction because there is no explanation on what access is neccesary, why is this access important. This creates an in-text contradiction, because the text abruptly jumps creating uncertanty on the topic of accesibility that is necessary for maintance of equipment, now its uncertain what the MA needs to maintain such equipment. What happens now is unclear, and its difficult to enforce any maintenance because of the uncertainty of this clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.6 Each Party that has designed or procured equipment used in the APCN 2 shall give necessary information relating to the operation and maintenance of such equipment to the MA responsible for operating and maintaining such equipment, as reflected in this Paragraph 10. if the MA responsible, as specified in this Paragraph 10, fails to restore those facilities to efficient working order and operation within a reasonable time after having been called upon to do so by any other Party to whom capacity is assigned by this Agreement, the Management Committee may, to the extent that it is practical to do so, place, or cause to be placed, such facilities in efficient working order and operation and charge the Parties their proportionate shares of the costs reasonably incurred in doing so.
perturbation.justification: The omission of the sentence 'Each Maintenance Authority shall have prompt access necessary for the performance of its 10.7 [Missing] interruption.' creates a significant gap in the explanation of the MA's responsibilities and rights regarding access. This lack of clarity directly impacts the enforceability of the agreement because it becomes unclear what 'prompt access necessary for the performance' actually entails. The contradicted text, which is the same as the original, now lacks the context of what constitutes appropriate access, potentially leading to disputes about whether the MA has been given sufficient means to perform its duties.

perturbation.original_text: 12.1 Capacity of APCN 2 can only be used by a carrier. 12.2 Parties shall obtain Allocated Capacity in the form of MIU on an ownership basis as shown in Schedule C, in return for their financial investment as identified in Schedule B. 12.3 Procedures for Parties activation of their Allocated Capacity will be developed by the AR&RSC and the NA for Management Committee approval. 24 ASSIGNMENT OF CAPACITY 12.4 For each MIU of its Allocated Capacity, a Party is allowed to assign two (2) half STM-l circuits on any Path within the APCN 2. All such circuits are ring-protected. Additional ring-protected half STM-1 circuits may be granted proportionately to a Party's Allocated Capacity as shown in Schedule C at the discretion of the Management Committee according to the recommendation of the AR&RSC and the NA.
perturbation.changed_text: 12.1 Capacity of APCN 2 can only be used by a carrier. 12.2 Parties shall obtain Allocated Capacity in the form of MIU on an ownership basis as shown in Schedule C, in return for their financial investment as identified in Schedule B. 24 ASSIGNMENT OF CAPACITY 12.4 For each MIU of its Allocated Capacity, a Party is allowed to assign two (2) half STM-l circuits on any Path within the APCN 2. All such circuits are ring-protected. Additional ring-protected half STM-1 circuits may be granted proportionately to a Party's Allocated Capacity as shown in Schedule C at the discretion of the Management Committee according to the recommendation of the AR&RSC and the NA.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting '12.3 Procedures for Parties activation of their Allocated Capacity will be developed by the AR&RSC and the NA for Management Committee approval.', the text creates an in-text contradiction. While the agreement establishes a framework for capacity allocation and assignment through MIUs, there are no specified methods to activate such allocated capacity. Without activation procedures, the entire framework of allocation, assignment, joint assignment, wholly assigned circuits, portability, transfer, and eventual sale/IRU is ambiguous, since parties do not know how to actívate it. Since there is not an established method of activation it causes ambiguity, making difficult to enforce.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12.4 For each MIU of its Allocated Capacity, a Party is allowed to assign two (2) half STM-l circuits on any Path within the APCN 2. All such circuits are ring-protected. Additional ring-protected half STM-1 circuits may be granted proportionately to a Party's Allocated Capacity as shown in Schedule C at the discretion of the Management Committee according to the recommendation of the AR&RSC and the NA.
perturbation.justification: The omission of clause '12.3 Procedures for Parties activation of their Allocated Capacity will be developed by the AR&RSC and the NA for Management Committee approval.' directly contradicts the functionality outlined in clause 12.4. Without the procedure for activating allocated capacity, the ability to assign circuits as described in 12.4 becomes ambiguous and unenforceable, as parties cannot operationalize their allocated capacity. The original text provides a mechanism for capacity assignment, but the changed text removes the means of activating that capacity, making the assignment described in 12.4 impossible. Therefore, there is a strong in-text contradiction.
